title: Minimum days to make M bouquets

content.problemStatement:
You are given 'N’ roses and you are also given an array 'arr'  where 'arr[i]'  denotes that the 'ith' rose will bloom on the 'arr[i]th' day.
You can only pick already bloomed roses that are adjacent to make a bouquet. You are also told that you require exactly 'k' adjacent bloomed roses to make a single bouquet.
Find the minimum number of days required to make at least ‘m' bouquets each containing 'k' roses. Return -1 if it is not possible.
content.inputFormat: The input will contain the following: an integer array 'arr' where 'arr[i]' denotes the day the 'i-th' rose will bloom, an integer 'N' representing the total number of roses, an integer 'm' representing the number of bouquets to be made, and an integer 'k' representing the number of adjacent roses required to make a bouquet.
content.outputFormat: The output will be the minimum number of days required to make at least 'm' bouquets, each containing 'k' roses. If it's not possible to make 'm' bouquets, the output will be -1.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a binary search approach. We can start by finding the minimum and maximum possible days when all roses can bloom. Then, we can perform a binary search between these two days to find the minimum day when we can make at least 'm' bouquets.
